---
title: "HVDC Export Cables"
---

## Role in the Value Chain

HVDC export cables transport electrical energy from the offshore converter station to the onshore converter station.

## Cost Model

| Item | Assumption |
|---|---:|
| Export cable capacity | 2 GW |
| Export cables installed in parallel | 1 |
| Installed cost benchmark | 7 million EUR/km |
| Installation cost | 635 EUR/m |
| Implied cable CAPEX | 6,365 EUR/m |
| OPEX | 2% of CAPEX per year |

The material CAPEX is derived by subtracting the modelled installation cost from the installed-cost benchmark.

## Energy Balance

| Component | Efficiency |
|---|---:|
| HVDC export cable | 99.5% |

## Availability

| Component | Availability |
|---|---:|
| HVDC export cable | 99.0% |

## Installation

Cable installation is modelled separately from material CAPEX. See [Cable Installation](../offshore_installation/cable_installation.qmd).

## Sizing Drivers

Export cable sizing is driven by rated export capacity and distance to shore. The current baseline distance to shore is 80 km.
